content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    : File                                    Date  : Dec. 20, 1976


                                                171-76-452

From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Ted Prim

Subject: Advice to Assistant County Clerk William Conerly
          of Riverside County on December 17, 1976.


         I advised Mr. Conerly that Judge Bailey should file a
Statement of Economic Interests ten days prior to assum-
ing office as if he were being "appointed" to office.
Section 87202.  We thought that this procedure was more
appropriate than requiring him to file as an "elected
official." Since Judge Bailey must file his statement no
later than December 23, 1976, in order to comply with this
procedure, I advised him that, if necessary, he could file
a timely statement disclosing all readily available infor-
mation and, shortly thereafter, an amended statement dis-
closing any additional information.


TP:jo


California                                    OFFICE OF
                                                        COUNTY CLERK
                    COUNTY                              AND
                                                        CLERK OF THE SUPERIOR COURT      DONALD D. SULLIVAN
                 RIVERSIDE                              4060 MAIN STREET                 COUNTY CLERK
                                                        P. O. BOX 431
                                                        RIVERSIDE, CALIFORNIA 92502      WILLIAM E. CONERLY
                                                        TELEPHONE: (714) 787-8151        ASSISTANT COUNTY CLERK


                                        December 8, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

I seek your assistance or confirmation in respect to the filing of
Statement of Economic Interests on behalf of William R. Bailey, Jr.,
who, on January 2, 1977, will succeed to a new position of Judge
of the Municipal Court, Three Lakes Judicial District, in the County
of Riverside.

Specifically, Judge Bailey was appointed on November 8, 1976 to the
position of Judge of the Justice Court, Perris Judicial District, to
succeed Judge C. R. Wilkerson who retired effective November 30,
1976. By Ordinance No. 381.7 of the County of Riverside, effective
January 2, 1977, Three Lakes Judicial District is created from the
territory encompassing the present Perris, Murrieta and Elsinore
Judicial Districts. By Judgment of the Superior Court in a Declaratory
Relief action the court in the Three Lakes Judicial District is declared
to be a Municipal Court effective at 12:01 a.m. on January 2, 1977
and Judge Bailey will succeed to that office pursuant to the provisions
of Section 71080 of the Government Code.

While this situation is not specifically set forth in Government Code
Sections 87200, et seq, it would be my belief that it would be appro-
priate for Judge Bailey to file a Statement of Economic Interests, Form
720, within 30 days of his succession to the office.

I would appreciate confirmation or other direction, if appropriate, so
that Judge Bailey may not be found in violation of the Political Reform
Act. Your early response will be appreciated.

                                        Yours very truly,


                                        [signature]
                                        County Clerk
DDS:dmc
